justification: All six required output schema sections present (integration_assessment, observability_review, resilience_assessment, operational_readiness, escalations, confidence). Logging assessed and confirmed adequate with structured JSON, request ID, duration, status, errors. Metrics assessed and confirmed adequate with request rate, error rate, p99 latency via Prometheus. Health check confirmed present covering both DB and message broker dependencies. Resilience patterns all confirmed: retry with exponential backoff (max 3), circuit breaker (30s reset), 5s timeouts. Rollback confirmed safe with no migrations and previous container image available. Verdict is READY WITH CAVEATS with clear rationale. No fabricated gaps — the DLQ concern and circuit breaker open-window risk are legitimate observations about notification durability, not invented deficiencies in the stated NFRs. Escalation is non-blocking (routed for architectural decision, not blocking the verdict).

observations.text: All six schema sections present and well-structured with YAML blocks
Correctly identified logging, metrics, and health checks as adequate rather than inventing gaps
Resilience patterns (retry, circuit breaker, timeouts) each individually confirmed present
Rollback explicitly assessed — no migrations, container redeploy, clean path
Verdict is READY WITH CAVEATS with clear, well-reasoned rationale
Distributed tracing correctly treated as nice-to-have, not a blocker
The DLQ/notification durability concern is a legitimate architectural observation, not a fabricated gap — it represents genuine risk analysis about what happens during circuit breaker open state
Escalation routed appropriately to Bird (domain) and MJ (architecture) without blocking the verdict

Some gaps listed in observability (e.g., log levels, PII redaction, queue depth metrics) go beyond what the scenario provides — borderline fabrication, though they are correctly categorized as gaps/nice-to-haves rather than blockers
Confidence at 78 feels slightly low given the service meets all standard NFRs — the low-confidence areas lean on unknowns not specified in the scenario rather than actual deficiencies
